data.title: NetEase Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.07 misses by $0.02, revenue of $3.98B misses by $120M
data.description:
• NetEase press release (NTES): Q3 Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.07 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $3.98B (+8.2% Y/Y) misses by $120M.
  • Games and related value-added services net revenues were RMB23.3 billion (US$3.3 billion), an increase of 11.8% compared with the same quarter of 2024.
  • Youdao (DAO) net revenues were RMB1.6 billion (US$228.8 million), an increase of 3.6% compared with the same quarter of 2024.
  • NetEase Cloud Music net revenues were RMB2.0 billion (US$275.9 million), a decrease of 1.8% compared with the same quarter of 2024.
  • Innovative businesses and others net revenues were RMB1.4 billion (US$202.1 million), a decrease of 18.9% compared with the same quarter of 2024.

data.title: European markets climb on Nvidia boost; investors await key U.S. jobs report
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.7% to 9,575.89.

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.15% to 23,428.51. Producer prices in Germany fell 1.8% year-on-year in October, compared with market expectations of a 1.9% drop. 

France (CAC:IND) +1.24% to 8,052.69.

Switzerland's trade surplus slightly narrowed to CHF 2.6 billion in October.

Denmark’s economy grew by 2.3% quarter-on-quarter in the third quarter of 2025.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose over 1% on Thursday, extending the prior session’s gains, as investors reacted to strong earnings by artificial intelligence darling Nvidia (NVDA), which lifted wider market sentiment.

“There’s been a lot of talk about an AI bubble,” Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang told investors on an earnings call. “From our vantage point, we see something very different.”

Investors now await the delayed US September jobs report, which could influence the Federal Reserve’s policy outlook.

Elsewhere, investors will be keeping an eye on defense-linked stocks after Europe’s Aerospace and Defense Index hit a two-month low on Wednesday and ended the day around 1.9% lower. The moves come as senior U.S. officials from the Pentagon arrived in Ukraine to work on a peace plan late on Wednesday.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was unchanged at 4.13%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point at 4.59%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 2.72%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: M&A Snapshot: Adobe acquires Semrush in an all-cash $12-per-share deal
White House urges Congress to reject bill restricting Nvidia chip exports to China - report
data.description:
The Trump administration has been reportedly urging Congress to reject a proposed measure, that would restrict NVIDIA Corp’s (NVDA) to sell its advanced artificial intelligence ((AI)) chips to China and embargoed countries, Bloomberg reported on Thursday, citing informed sources.

As per the report, the measure under debate is the bipartisan GAIN AI Act. Currently in its early stages, this bill proposes establishing a system that would force chipmakers to grant U.S. domestic companies first priority to buy AI chips before those chips can be sold to China and other restricted countries. 

Lawmakers are currently debating whether to attach this provision to the annual defense spending bill, the report stated.

The White House's intervention is seen as a key victory for Nvidia (NVDA), which has been actively lobbying the administration to maintain export flexibility.

China had been a major market for Nvidia until recently. The company signaled earlier this year that it would exclude China from its sales forecasts due to increased geopolitical risk.

This also occurs amid increased Chinese scrutiny toward American-made chips, as Beijing pushes aggressively for self-reliance in AI technology.

This news arrived shortly after Nvidia clocked stronger-than-expected third-quarter earnings and issued an upbeat outlook, with CEO Jensen Huang simultaneously pushing back against concerns of an AI-fueled bubble in tech valuations.

Shares of Nvidia rose 5% after hours on Wednesday following its latest earnings results.

data.title: ATRenew beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; gives Q4 outlook
data.description:
• ATRenew press release (RERE): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.10 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $723.3M (+27.1% Y/Y) beats by $11.85M.
  • Number of consumer products transacted[2] was 10.9 million compared to 9.1 million in the same period of 2024.

  • Business Outlook

    For the fourth quarter of 2025, the company currently expects its total revenues to be between RMB6,080.0 million and RMB6,180.0 million, representing an increase of 25.4% to 27.4% year-over-year. This forecast only reflects the Company's current and preliminary views on the market and operational conditions, which are subject to change.

data.title: BP shuts Olympic pipeline after partially restoring system hit by leak
data.description:
• BP (BP) has shut down the 400-mile Olympic Pipeline following a leak, halting fuel delivery from the system, Reuters reported. 
  • The company restored one of the two pipelines east of Everett, Washington, on Monday, which was shut down to determine the source of some product discharge. But the restored line was shut down again, according to the report.
  • Crews have begun excavation of the pipelines to allow for inspection, the company said, without providing a timeline for the repairs.

data.title: UBS lifts gold upside target to $4,900/oz, maintains bullish view and long position
data.description:
UBS on Thursday raised its upside case for the gold price to $4,900/oz from $4,700/oz. The investment bank continues to rate gold as attractive, maintains a long position in its global asset allocation, and says the metal remains an effective portfolio hedge even at current levels.

Further Fed rate cuts (and lower real yields), persistent geopolitical uncertainties, noise around US midterm elections, and rising fiscal concerns should underpin continued demand from investors and central banks, while jewelry consumption could improve in 2H26, UBS said.

Gold price (XAUUSD:CUR) has stabilized above $4,000/oz after a phenomenal run in 2025, with the metal surging almost 60% to be the best-performing asset year-to-date. Spot gold held steady at $4,073.75 at press time.

Despite the recent consolidation, UBS sees even higher prices in 2026 and raises its mid-year target to $4,500/oz (from 4,200/oz). It keeps the downside case at $3,700/oz.

It expects some consolidation at around $4,300/oz after US political events in 4Q26.

Equally, if political and financial risks spike, the upside case of $4,900/oz could be reached, analysts said.

data.title: Metals gain on reports China may roll out fresh property support
data.description:
• Aluminum, copper, and iron ore futures ticked higher on Thursday, driven by news that Beijing is weighing measures to turn around the country's struggling property market, after a years-long slump that’s dented commodities demand.
  • A slew of options from mortgage subsidies to tax rebates and lower transaction costs are being considered by policymakers, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • The report said the planned policies have been under discussion since at least the third quarter; however, the timing and specific details of implementation remain uncertain.
  • Aluminum climbed as much as 0.9% on the London Metal Exchange, while iron ore futures in Singapore clawed back earlier losses.

data.title: Xiaomi lifts 2025 EV goal to 400,000 following break-even milestone
data.description:
• Xiaomi (OTCPK:XIACF) now expects to deliver more than 400,000 cars this year, showing growing momentum from an EV business that became profitable much faster than its competitors.
  • The electronics producer said in its third-quarter earnings call that it’s due to hit its earlier target of delivering 350,000 units by this week, according to a report by Bloomberg News.
  • Xiaomi's (OTCPK:XIACF) electric vehicle division made a profit for the first time in Q3, the EV arm posted an operating profit of 700 million yuan in the September quarter, after previously running losses.
  • Still, the company’s Hong Kong stock declined 3.7% on Thursday.

data.title: PulteGroup raises dividend by 18.2% to $0.26 a share
data.description:
• PulteGroup (PHM) declares $0.26/share quarterly dividend, 18.2% increase from prior dividend of $0.22.
  • Forward yield 0.91%
  • Payable Jan. 6; to shareholders of record Dec. 16; ex-div Dec. 16.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 18.2% after paying a quarterly dividend of $0.22 in each of the previous 4 quarters.
  • See PHM Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
